decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at 500.0 W/(m*K). However, since this is already at the upper bound defined in the editable variables, I will instead increase the heat spreader width to improve lateral heat spreading area. A moderate increase from 0.7m to 0.9m (within the 1.5x step limit) will help distribute heat more effectively towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the lateral conduction path, allowing heat to reach the Dirichlet boundary conditions on the left and right walls more efficiently, thereby reducing the peak temperature in the chip.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
Improved heat flux towards the side boundaries
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the domain edges; ensure it does not exceed the design domain width of 1.2m (current x0=0.25, new width 0.9 -> x1=1.15, which is valid).
Diminishing returns may occur if the spreader becomes too wide relative to the heat source size, but this step is conservative.
